title: GHOST CREW — ECHO DIRECTOR'S CUT V8 RULE-30 PASS

events.text: I hate that those words make sense.
I don't want to go in there.
Which means we probably have to.

event.speaker: HALE
event.text: Maya, if the archive still has power, assume it still has teeth.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: Hold on. Something shifted under the panel.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: No. I'm moving. Not testing my luck by standing still.

events.text: If I go quiet, I'm trying not to become part of the floor.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: Cable touched my neck. I handled it normally. Very normally.

events.text: If I go quiet, I'm stuck or swearing quietly.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: I thought I heard something behind the hatch.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: No. Just the relay pack settling. I think.

events.text: Panel gives me two bad options.
Or spend a minute making sure it doesn't bite.
I am choosing not to touch the angry metal.
Hale, if this thing takes my fingerprints, I am blaming you later.
events.speaker: MAYA

event.speaker: HALE
event.text: Careful is not cowardly. I know that ruins Maya's entire brand, but there it is.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: I heard that.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: Hold on. Something moved between the cabinets.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: No. Hanging label. It swung when I stepped in. I hate this room.

events.text: Clean rectangle where one used to be.

events.title: PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION - ETHAN HALE
events.content:
Subject: Ethan Hale
Status: Stable
Risk Level: Low
Recommendation: Active Duty Approved
Evaluator Note: Subject demonstrates strong command resilience under isolation pressure.
Created: 12 April
Linked Incident: Alpha
events.caption: Recovered psychological evaluation record. Timestamp appears inconsistent.

events.text: I once yelled at a printer until Grant left the room.
events.speaker: HALE

events.title: PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION - MAYA CHEN
events.content:
Subject: Maya Chen
Status: Stable
Risk Level: Low
Recommendation: Active Duty Approved
Evaluator Note: Subject shows high pattern-recognition ability and repeated resistance to memory suppression protocols.
Created: 12 April
Linked Incident: Alpha
events.caption: Recovered psychological evaluation record. Note contains memory suppression reference.

events.text: Nobody told us there were memory protocols.
And someone filed it like medicine.
Before my head softens the edges.
And if you opened mine first...
If I spiral, talk to me like I'm fixing something.

events.title: PSYCH EVALUATION CONFLICT SUMMARY
events.content:
HALE: Marked stable after Incident Alpha.
MAYA: Repeated resistance to memory suppression protocols.
VOSS: Archive slot exists, record body removed.
INDEX: Archive created after Incident Alpha date.

Conflict: Evaluations appear to be post-incident control records, not pre-incident wellness assessments.
events.caption: Combined evaluation conflict summary generated from recovered records.
events.message: [EVALUATION CONFLICT SUMMARY ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: They weren't checking if we were okay.
They needed the file to say we were.
And if Hale gets calm again, remember this room.

event.speaker: HALE
event.text: I can hear you, Maya.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: Good. Then listen better.

events.text: That sounds like something people say when they want control.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Some systems wake up when you look at them.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: This archive is acting like one of them.
events.speaker: HALE

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: You hear that? He made fear sound like advice.

event.speaker: HALE
event.text: I heard that too.

events.text: Easier for the archive to lie through.
Original index drive is right there.
Machines should not pause like that.

event.speaker: HALE
event.text: If it asks twice, read the second prompt. Old systems hide knives in confirmation screens.

events.text: I can feel it through the glove.
If I go quiet, both hands are on live metal.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: Wait. I thought I saw a face in the cabinet glass.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: No. Mine. Reflection. Hate that face right now.

events.status: HAS BOTH HANDS ON THE DRIVE
events.message: [MAYA HAS BOTH HANDS ON THE DRIVE]

events.title: GHOST CREW COUNT REPORT
events.content:
OFFICIAL MANIFEST COUNT: 6
RECOVERED MANIFEST LAYER COUNT: 7
ORIGINAL ASSIGNMENT INDEX COUNT: 8

Discrepancy: one person removed from official manifest. One additional person removed from recovered manifest layer.
Visual archive unavailable. Count derived from personnel hashes, suit registry keys and life support allocation records.
events.caption: Personnel count reconstructed without image records.
events.message: [GHOST CREW COUNT REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: I need that to be wrong.
And I don't like how close that feels to a memory.
events.speaker: HALE

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: Operator... sealed subfolder under the eighth slot.

choices.text: SEAL IT AND GET MAYA OUT.

events.text: If this is a mistake, it belongs to both of us.
Because part of me is relieved.
And I don't know what that says about me.

event.speaker: UNKNOWN
event.text: SEALING

event.speaker: UNKNOWN
event.text: IS NOT

event.speaker: UNKNOWN
event.text: FORGETTING

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: I think something opened a drawer behind me.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: Not looking.

events.text: If I ask to go back in, say no.
If I get angry, still say no.
I am so tired of things remembering for us.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Then we go to the pod.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You always say that right before everything gets worse.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: This time I am hoping to be wrong.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Found scorch marks on the spine.

events.title: DIRECT SPINE BURN TRACE
events.content:
DIRECT RELAY SPINE TRACE
Residual heat pattern detected near archive access.
Pattern shape: emergency pod transfer conduit.
Age estimate: recent / post-blackout.
Note: Route was used after official shutdown.
events.caption: Heat trace found on the direct spine. Suggests pod-related activity after the blackout.
events.message: [DIRECT SPINE BURN TRACE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: There are boot prints in the dust.
Someone came through after power failed.
I hate that I had to add that.

events.title: CRAWLSPACE DRAG TRACE
events.content:
SERVICE CRAWL TRACE
Boot pattern: unknown
Drag mark: narrow, intermittent
Direction: archive access hatch → auxiliary pod conduit
Timestamp: post-blackout estimate
events.caption: Physical trace discovered in the service crawlspace. Suggests someone moved through after power loss.
events.message: [CRAWLSPACE DRAG TRACE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: The kind that pretends it's furniture.
Hale was right about the second prompt.
Remote report has a checksum gap.
It has a raw assignment hash.

events.title: RAW ASSIGNMENT HASH
events.content:
ORIGINAL INDEX DRIVE HASH
Slot 08: active key preserved
Name field: stripped
Pod conduit key: present
Archive mirror: absent
Note: Raw index predates psych archive overwrite.
events.caption: Raw assignment hash recovered only by physically removing the index drive.
events.message: [RAW ASSIGNMENT HASH ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.title: EIGHTH SLOT FRAGMENT
events.content:
Assignment Index Slot: 08
Name Field: CORRUPTED
Suit Registry Echo: NV-08 adjacent
Pod Link: External emergency pod
Access Note: DO NOT RESTORE WHILE SUBJECT IS BREATHING
events.caption: Fragment recovered from the sealed eighth assignment slot. Opening this record exposed the pod link.
events.message: [EIGHTH SLOT FRAGMENT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Lights just came on behind me.
Don't ask me to make that make sense.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I am trying not to remember something that wants to be remembered.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Which makes me trust it less.

events.title: SEALED EIGHTH SLOT TRACE
events.content:
SEALED SLOT PRESSURE TRACE
Slot: 08
Name field: inaccessible
External link: emergency pod conduit
Safety state: sealed / not awakened
Note: Opening deferred. Trace preserved without activating archive response.
events.caption: Trace preserved by sealing the eighth slot instead of opening it. The pod link is visible, but the name remains hidden.
events.message: [SEALED EIGHTH SLOT TRACE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Something knocked inside the archive after I crossed the hatch.
Like drawers opening one at a time.
You told me to open it.
I'm not saying that like blame.
I'm saying it because I need you to remember.
Or saved a person from being woken wrong.

upgradeFocus: Sharpened relationship residue after opening Maya first or hearing Hale out.
Made remote cross-check vs index drive a real information trade-off.
Made open eighth slot vs seal eighth slot a real irreversible consequence.
Strengthened final hook toward the pod.

episodeObjective: Guide Maya through the personnel archive, expose post-incident psychological control records, choose between safer remote proof and dangerous original evidence, then decide whether to open or seal the eighth slot before the pod signal forces Episode 08.

summary: First event, then reaction, then choice.
Maya talks like a survivor under pressure, not a narrator explaining lore.
Choices now leave visible emotional residue.
Return nodes report what happened through lived reaction, not clean summaries.
The archive, route, drive, eighth slot, and final pod hook are preserved.
UNKNOWN is more corrupted and less polished.
Hale and Maya maintain distinct voices and relationship tension.

rulesApplied: Concrete objective: reach personnel archive, recover control records, resolve the eighth slot.
Progress can be movement, searching, hiding, arguing, stabilizing, or extracting evidence; every scene changes information, risk, relationship, or route state.
Every visible choice has exactly two options.
Route choices create visible physical and evidence consequences.
Report-style action lines were rewritten into lived actions and physical reactions.
CharacterBusy is reserved for meaningful off-screen physical work and shortened for better pacing.
Maya retains humor but it is reduced when fear, injury, or betrayal should dominate.
Hale stays ambiguous rather than fully guilty or fully safe.
UNKNOWN pushes plot: absence count, eighth slot, pod memory.
Episode ends with a strong Pod Seven / Pod Eight hook for Episode 08.

episode08UsageNotes.mayaPersonalBoundaryCrossed: Maya should remain clipped and guarded if her evaluation was opened first.
episode08UsageNotes.openedHaleFirst: Hale feels exposed first; he should deflect less cleanly and show fear around April 12.
episode08UsageNotes.openedArchiveFast: Maya has a scorched sleeve and references the operator choosing speed over safety.
episode08UsageNotes.archiveScanned: Maya gives the operator more benefit of the doubt on safety calls.
episode08UsageNotes.originalHashMissed: Remote route gives incomplete truth; Episode 08 can withhold or corrupt a later confirmation.
episode08UsageNotes.mayaHandBurned: Drive route makes Maya's hand pain visible in Episode 08.
episode08UsageNotes.eighthSlotOpened: Archive is awake and may actively interfere.
episode08UsageNotes.eighthSlotSealed: Maya is safer but carries guilt about leaving a name behind.
episode08UsageNotes.podSignalFollows: Open channel makes Pod Eight interference stronger.
episode08UsageNotes.podSignalArchived: Cut channel lowers immediate danger but leaves less live signal.
